segments.text: Then Job answered and said,
How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: These ten times have ye reproached me: ye are not ashamed that ye make yourselves strange to me.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: make yourselves strange to me: or, harden yourselves against me

segments.text: And be it indeed that I have erred, mine error remaineth with myself.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If indeed ye will magnify yourselves against me, and plead against me my reproach:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Know now that God hath overthrown me, and hath compassed me with his net.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard: I cry aloud, but there is no judgment.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.
He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.
He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.
He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as one of his enemies.
His troops come together, and raise up their way against me, and encamp round about my tabernacle.
He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.
My kinsfolk have failed, and my familiar friends have forgotten me.
They that dwell in mine house, and my maids, count me for a stranger: I am an alien in their sight.
I called my servant, and he gave me no answer; I intreated him with my mouth.
My breath is strange to my wife, though I intreated for the children's sake of mine own body.

footnotes: mine own body: Heb. my belly

segments.text: Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.
All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.

footnotes: my inward friends: Heb. the men of my secret

segments.text: My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.
Have pity upon me, have pity upon me, O ye my friends; for the hand of God hath touched me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book!
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: Oh that: Heb. Who will give, etc

segments.text: That they were graven with an iron pen and lead in the rock for ever!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For I know that my redeemer liveth, and that he shall stand at the latter day upon the earth:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: And though after my skin worms destroy this body, yet in my flesh shall I see God:
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: And though: or, After I shall awake, though this body be destroyed, yet out of my flesh

segments.text: Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another; though my reins be consumed within me.
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: though my reins be consumed within me: or, my reins within me are consumed with earnest desire (for that day)
within me: Heb. in my bosom

segments.text: But ye should say, Why persecute we him, seeing the root of the matter is found in me?
segments.speaker: Job

footnotes: seeing the root of the matter is found in me: or, and what root of matter is found in me?

segments.text: Be ye afraid of the sword: for wrath bringeth the punishments of the sword, that ye may know there is a judgment.
segments.speaker: Job
